column18:
Welcome to the research study!  

 



We are interested in better understanding the nature of dreams.  You will be presented with information relevant to the self-reporting of dreams and some mental health measures. Please be assured that your responses will be kept completely confidential. This survey is one of a series of five. Following participation in this survey, you will be invited to a follow-up survey at 1 month, 2 months, 3 months, and 4 months – all your responses will be linked so that trends can be tracked over time. You will be paid for each follow-up survey. Following the completion of all surveys, there will be a $1.00 bonus. 

The survey should take 15 minutes to complete. After completing the survey, you will be paid $3.00 for your participation. The following surveys will take less time, and you will be paid an additional $2.00 for each follow-up survey taken. The final survey with the bonus ($1.00 + $2.00) will pay you $3.00. We do not ask for your name or any other information that might identify you. Although collected data may be made public or used for future research purposes, your identity will always remain confidential.


 The research will not benefit you personally. We know of no risks resulting from participating in the study. Your participation is voluntary. You may withdraw at any time, and you may choose not to answer any question, but you must proceed to the final screen of the study in order to receive your completion code which you must submit in order to be paid. In accordance with [online survey site] policies, we may reject your work if the HIT was not completed correctly or the instructions were not followed. Your participation in this research is voluntary. You have the right to withdraw at any point during the study, for any reason, and without any prejudice. 

 If you have any questions you can contact me at pcm38@duke.edu, or my advisor at ws66@duke.edu. If you have questions about your rights as a research subject, contact Duke’s Campus Institutional Review Board at campusirb@duke.edu or at 919-684-3030, please refer to protocol #2024-0062. By clicking the button below, you acknowledge that your participation in the study is voluntary, you are 18 years of age, and that you are aware that you may choose to terminate your participation in the study at any time and for any reason.

I had a dream that my favorite video game was actually my life. All of the characters in the game were different people from my immediate social circle. I was a samurai, and I was fighting the Mongols who were invading my island. (The game is ghost of tsushima). It wasn't at all like the game though, as I refused to use poison against people, I refused to use any weapon or technique that would cause anyone unnecessary pain and I ALWAYS attempted to speak to the leader of the group/camp I was attacking before I hurt any of them. I attempted to use words instead of weapons in order to give them the chance to go back to their homes and see their families again without suffering a senseless death. Every time I killed a camp or patrol of them, I buried them and said "a mongol patrol lies here, and no matter how you feel, they deserve peace and respect the same as you and i".

I woke up in the middle of paying my respects to a warlord I had fought in a duel and burried.. and I woke up pretty sad and depressed.

column50: My grandmother (who is still alive in real life) had just died. After attending the funeral mass, we were gathered at her house for a meal; as we sat around the table, the decor began to change back to what it had been before a remodel in the late 1990s; I was the only one who seemed to notice this. As the conversation went on I began to notice other people who were appearing, faces completely sullen with dead eyes; these were dead, distant relatives I normally only know from old black-and-white pictures. I became increasingly uneasy, but was alone among the living in noticing; meanwhile the dead seemed to phase in and out of existence, with some more and more recent. As my grandmother herself showed up I took it to mean that perhaps her spirit was here to partake in reminiscence which was ongoing at the dinner table. I pointed this out but got nothing but strange looks from everybody as she walked over. Somebody said "but that's not really her!" as she cried out demonically, contorting her mouth and revealing a total lack of eyes as the scream dropped in pitch to a demonic roar and she lunged at the group, as the dead in the other room turned to see what would surely have been a violent attack if I had not awoken at that moment. It is seared into my memory not only for being rather unpleasant, but because -- in retrospect -- it was a very bad omen for my father's routine surgery later that day, which actually turned out to be almost fatal itself.

column50:
I dreamed of a garage filled with stored maintenance supplies in the back. The building was multi-storied, and I lived on the top floor. I had climbed up the side of the building using exposed pipes to hold onto, and after climbing down went into the garage to look at the stuff in the back. The garage door was open, and a car pulled up with two guys, one jumped out to get something from the garage. He was Filipino, said "Hi" and so did I. We talked a little as he rummaged through the supplies. It was obvious the maintenance stuff belonged to him, and he said I could use it if I needed anything.
I remember feeling good as I shimmied up the pipes on the side of the building and climbed through a kitchen window of my flat. I don't remember anything after that.

column50: The most memorable one: I had a dream of playing in a basketball playoff game. It was the first round of the playoffs and I as playing against one of my closest friends in a rival high school. Back in the day, I was short and skinny. But in my dream, my body felt extremely light yet powerful. I started the game by retrieving the tip-off and scoring on a breakaway slam. However, the coach took me out of the game right after. I felt angry and confused at first but he insisted that he was going to use my as a weapon later on. Upon hearing those words, I felt invigorated and eagerly waited until the second quarter. When the starters showed signs of fatigue, he subbed me in for our captain, the point guard (player #17). The game resumed and I knocked down a three pointer almost immediately. I would go on to score the same way in 8 straight possessions.

column50:
In the dream, I find myself navigating through a dense forest illuminated by the soft moonlight filtering through the thick canopy. The atmosphere is serene, filled with the earthy scent of pine needles, and the trees seem to whisper secrets as I walk deeper into the woods. Suddenly, I stumble upon a clearing aglow with silver moonlight. At its heart stands an ancient oak tree, its gnarled branches reaching skyward like the fingers of an old sage. Intrigued, I cautiously approach the tree and extend my hand to touch its rough bark. Upon contact, a profound surge of energy courses through my body, enveloping me in a sense of wonder and awe. In that moment, I feel an inexplicable connection to something greater than myself, as if the tree is imparting ancient wisdom directly into my soul.

As I stand beneath the ethereal light of the moon, a gentle yet powerful voice echoes in my mind, speaking of balance, harmony, and the delicate dance between light and dark. Mesmerized, I listen intently, absorbing each word as if it were a precious gift bestowed upon me by the universe itself. Eventually, the voice fades away, leaving me standing alone in the clearing. 

With newfound purpose and clarity, I leave the clearing and continue on my journey, carrying the wisdom of the ancient oak tree with me wherever I go, forever changed by the profound experience of the dream.
